name: Armstrong, Joe Flynt
motto: "The women pardoned all except his face."
text: "The women pardoned all except his face."

name: Birchett, John Alexander Klein Jr.
motto: "Aye, every inch a king!"
clubs: Student Assistant in Bacteriology and Pathology
text: "Aye, every inch a king!"

name: Blanks, Dora Mae
motto: "Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace."
text: "Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace."

name: Brummett, Dale
motto:
"Her voice was ever soft,
Gentle and low—an excellent thing in woman."
text:
"Her voice was ever soft,
Gentle and low—an excellent thing in woman."

name: Butler, Raymond Gattis
motto: "Greater men than I may have lived, but I doubt it."
text: "Greater men than I may have lived, but I doubt it."

name: Campbell, May
motto: "A sweet, attractive kind of grace, A full assurance given by looks."
text: "A sweet, attractive kind of grace, A full assurance given by looks."

name: Downer, Charles L.
motto: "He hath indeed bettered expectations."
text: "He hath indeed bettered expectations."

name: Du Berry, John R.
motto: "What is a man, if he is not a mystery?"
clubs: Vice-President of A. S. B. O.
text: "What is a man, if he is not a mystery?"

name: Friedman, Florrie
motto: "But, at her smile, the bold revived again."
description: Lexington, Miss. Stigler, Okla. Oxford, Miss.
text:
"But, at her smile, the bold revived again."
Lexington, Miss. Stigler, Okla. Oxford, Miss.

name: Martin, Hemphill
text: Assistant in Biology.

name: Robert, Curry Jacob
motto: "I was not always a man of woe."
description: "A youth of labor with an age of ease." "I leave my character behind me." "A hungry, lean-faced villain." "A man made to be loved."
text:
"I was not always a man of woe."
"A youth of labor with an age of ease." "I leave my character behind me." "A hungry, lean-faced villain." "A man made to be loved."

name: Jeter, Ellie
motto: "Frailty, thy name is woman."
text: "Frailty, thy name is woman."

name: Jobe, E. R.
motto: "A little, fat, oily man of God."
text: "A little, fat, oily man of God."

name: Klingman, E. E. Jr.
motto: "Sleep to me, of all things else, is dear."
clubs: Y. M. C. A., Four Years Freshman Latin
text: "Sleep to me, of all things else, is dear."

name: Kyle, Hudson
motto:
"Fit to bear
The weight of mightiest monarchies."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Council, '16-'17, Cabinet. '17-'18
text:
"Fit to bear
The weight of mightiest monarchies."

name: Latimer, Hosford
motto:
"Whose harshest ideas
Will to melody run."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Representative to Convention, '16-'17
text:
"Whose harshest ideas
Will to melody run."

name: Mounger, Henry, Jr.
motto: "Something between a hindrance and a help."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Junior Class, '17-'18
text: "Something between a hindrance and a help."

name: McCain, Hugh Mosby
motto: "Every man to his own trade; then would the cows be well cared for."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Cabinet and Council, '16-'17, '17-'18
text: "Every man to his own trade; then would the cows be well cared for."

name: McDonald, Elizabeth
motto:
"A daughter of the gods, divinely tall,
And most divinely fair."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Senior Class
text:
"A daughter of the gods, divinely tall,
And most divinely fair."

name: McDonald, H. D.
motto: "And so let men conduct themselves in life as to be strangers to defeat."
text: "And so let men conduct themselves in life as to be strangers to defeat."

name: McRaney, E. B.
motto: "My valor is certainly going; I feel it sneaking off."
text: "My valor is certainly going; I feel it sneaking off."

name: Moffitt, M. E.
motto: "Beholding the bright countenance of truth in the quiet and still air of delightful studies."
text: "Beholding the bright countenance of truth in the quiet and still air of delightful studies."

name: McKinstry, George C.
motto:
"True as the needle to the pole,
Or the dial to the sun."
text:
"True as the needle to the pole,
Or the dial to the sun."

name: Pigford, W. L.
motto: "As happy as the day is long."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Red and Blue-Elect
text: "As happy as the day is long."

name: Ramey, Marvel
motto: "Is she not passing fair?"
text: "Is she not passing fair?"

name: Ridgeway, J. E.
motto:
"A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays,
And confident tomorrow."
text:
"A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays,
And confident tomorrow."

name: Scales, Charles M., Jr.
motto:
"His hair is just grizzled,
As in green old age,"
text:
"His hair is just grizzled,
As in green old age,"

name: Schauber, Alice
motto:
" So didst thou travel on life's common way,
In cheerful Godliness. "
text:
" So didst thou travel on life's common way,
In cheerful Godliness. "

name: Schreir, Phil C.
motto: "Every man is the architect of his own fortune. "
text: "Every man is the architect of his own fortune. "

name: Sides, Annie Frost
motto: "Fair as the day, and sweet as May. "
description: Winner of Taylor Medal in French.
text:
"Fair as the day, and sweet as May. "
Winner of Taylor Medal in French.

name: Swan, Thomas H.
motto: "A proper man, as one shall see on a summer's day. "
text: "A proper man, as one shall see on a summer's day. "

name: Sisler, W. Horton
motto:
"He was the mildest-mannered man
That ever scuttled ship, or cut a throat."
text:
"He was the mildest-mannered man
That ever scuttled ship, or cut a throat."

name: Tisdale, Alfred Dent
motto: "Dost thou love life? Then do not squander time, for that is the stuff life is made of."
text:
"Dost thou love life? Then do not squander time, for that is the stuff life is made of."
Freshman Mississippi College, '13-'14.

name: Townsend, Virginia
motto:
"A form so fair that, like the air,
'Tis less of earth than heaven."
text:
"A form so fair that, like the air,
'Tis less of earth than heaven."

name: Woodward, Sam Bee
motto:
"He was a man, take him for all in all,
I shall not look upon his like again."
text:
"He was a man, take him for all in all,
I shall not look upon his like again."

name: Wright, Donald Stephenson
motto:
"His life was gentle; and the elements,
So mixed in him that Nature might stand up
And say to all the world, This was a man!"
text:
"His life was gentle; and the elements,
So mixed in him that Nature might stand up
And say to all the world, This was a man!"

name: Henry C. Yawn
motto: "He had but one idea, and that was wrong."
text: "He had but one idea, and that was wrong."

name: Joseph Patton Evans
motto:
"He was a man, take him all for all,
I shall not look upon his like again."
text:
"He was a man, take him all for all,
I shall not look upon his like again."

name: Fred Adams
motto: "Audacity as against modesty will win battle over most in n."
text: "Audacity as against modesty will win battle over most in n."

name: Daniel Rupert Johnson
motto: "A patient man's pattern for a king."
text: "A patient man's pattern for a king."

name: M. E. Moffitt
motto: "Ay, marry; now unmuzzle your wisdom."
text: "Ay, marry; now unmuzzle your wisdom."

name: Bennett, Terry Lee
motto: "What stronger breastplate than a heart untainted."
text: "What stronger breastplate than a heart untainted."

name: Boland, W. Tilden
motto: "With all appliances and means to boot."
text: "With all appliances and means to boot."

name: Duncan, Perry Emory
motto: "But I am constant as a Northern star, of whose true-fixed and resting quality There is no fellow in the firmament."
text: "But I am constant as a Northern star, of whose true-fixed and resting quality There is no fellow in the firmament."

name: Ewell, George H.
motto: "All Hell broke loose."
text: "All Hell broke loose."

name: Evans, Joseph Patton
motto: "Mark the perfect man and behold the upright."
text: "Mark the perfect man and behold the upright."

name: Field, Richard Jennings
motto: " Framed to make women false."
text: " Framed to make women false."

name: Field, Samuel Eugene
motto:
"Blest with temper, whose unclouded ray,
Can make tomorrow cheerful as today."
text:
"Blest with temper, whose unclouded ray,
Can make tomorrow cheerful as today."

name: Gravlee, Ike M.
motto: "They laugh that win."
text: "They laugh that win."

name: Harper, Sidney James, Jr.
motto: "Drink, pretty creature, drink."
text: "Drink, pretty creature, drink."

name: Hicks, William Wynne
motto: "Ay, that is study’s godlike recompense."
text: "Ay, that is study’s godlike recompense."

name: Jenkins, William Nathan
motto:
"Thou hast no sorrow in thy song.
No winter is in thy year."
major: B.S. Degree at Alabama Presbyterian College, M.D.
text:
"Thou hast no sorrow in thy song.
No winter is in thy year."

name: Lazo, J. Anselmo
motto: "Why, here is a change, indeed!"
text: "Why, here is a change, indeed!"

name: Linder, Felix Elijah
motto: "Upon his brow shame is ashamed to sit."
text: "Upon his brow shame is ashamed to sit."

name: May, William Ross
motto: "Joy arises in me, like a summer morn."
text: "Joy arises in me, like a summer morn."

name: McDonald, Herman Davis
motto: "A prodigy of learning."
text: "A prodigy of learning."

name: Moseley, Sam Oliphant
motto:
"He takes the strongest liberties,
But never takes his leave."
text:
"He takes the strongest liberties,
But never takes his leave."

name: Ridgeway, James E.
motto: "As plain as a pike's staff."
text: "As plain as a pike's staff."

name: Rigby, Owen Compton
motto: "I am not in the roll of common men."
clubs: Board of Control of Students' Co-operative Club
text: "I am not in the roll of common men."

name: Sparks, Thomas D.
motto: "All of the perfumes of Arabia will not sweeten this little lad."
text: "All of the perfumes of Arabia will not sweeten this little lad."

name: Sisler, Wade Horton
motto: "Worth makes the man."
text: "Worth makes the man."

name: Willis, Walter Lafayette
motto: "A wee, small voice."
text: "A wee, small voice."

name: Woodward, Samuel Bee
motto: "Spreading himself like a green bay tree."
text: "Spreading himself like a green bay tree."

name: Wyatt, Kon
motto: "While then the world's mine oyster, Which I, with sword, will open."
text: "While then the world's mine oyster, Which I, with sword, will open."

name: Elliott, R. E.
motto: "Honors achieved far exceed those that are created."
text: "Honors achieved far exceed those that are created."

name: Adams, F.
motto: "Fortune cannot take away what she did not give."
text: "Fortune cannot take away what she did not give."

name: Allen, P. W.
motto: "I know naught save friends and duty."
clubs: Board of Control Co-operative Boarding Club
text: "I know naught save friends and duty."

name: Bridges, Leroy Clifford
motto:
"Thou art weighed in the balance
And art found wanting."
text:
"Thou art weighed in the balance
And art found wanting."

name: Caldwell, J. H.
motto: "I am sober as a judge."
text: "I am sober as a judge."

name: Davis, Clifford
motto:
"Hear ye not the hum
Of mighty workings?"
text:
"Hear ye not the hum
Of mighty workings?"

name: Dunn, James Leslie
motto: "Damned to everlasting fame."
text: "Damned to everlasting fame."

name: David, B. Fried
motto: "It takes courage to be a lawyer."
text: "It takes courage to be a lawyer."

name: Robert, E. Lee Geisenberger
motto: "Who thinks too little, And who talks too much."
text: "Who thinks too little, And who talks too much."

name: Alexander, Z. Golden
motto: "The law, it has honored us: may we honor it."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Senior Law Class
text: "The law, it has honored us: may we honor it."

name: David, B. Holmes
motto: "That man that hath a tongue. I say, is no man If with his tongue he cannot win a woman."
text: "That man that hath a tongue. I say, is no man If with his tongue he cannot win a woman."

name: W., Matthew Hutto
motto: "God made him; therefore, let him pass for a man."
text: "God made him; therefore, let him pass for a man."

name: William C. McLean
motto: "Lord of thy presence, and no land beside."
text: "Lord of thy presence, and no land beside."

name: Niles Moseley
motto: "If he be not fellow of the best kings, thou shalt find the king of best fellows."
text: "If he be not fellow of the best kings, thou shalt find the king of best fellows."

name: David Moore
motto: "Men—some to business, some to pleasure, take."
text: "Men—some to business, some to pleasure, take."

name: Charles F. Patterson
motto: "He makes a solitude, and calls it peace."
text: "He makes a solitude, and calls it peace."

name: Sylvanus William Polk
motto:
"He thought as a sage,
But felt as a man."
text:
"He thought as a sage,
But felt as a man."
Distinction.

name: Edward, D. Sauls
motto: " As calm and as quiet as a summer Venetian night. "
text: " As calm and as quiet as a summer Venetian night. "

name: Joseph, C. Sheedy
motto: "A man's good character is the world's common legacy. "
text: "A man's good character is the world's common legacy. "

name: Arthur, Stovall
motto: "Fair tresses man's imperial race ensnare. And beauty draws with a single hair."
text: "Fair tresses man's imperial race ensnare. And beauty draws with a single hair."

name: John, L. Toler
motto: "Resolved to ruin or rule the State. "
text: "Resolved to ruin or rule the State. "

name: Bonney, Walter A.
motto: "Thou wear a lion's hide! Doff it for shame! And a hang a calf's skin on those recreant limbs."
text: "Thou wear a lion's hide! Doff it for shame! And a hang a calf's skin on those recreant limbs."

name: Dunn, William Wheeler
motto: "Lord, we know what we are, but know not what we may be."
text: "Lord, we know what we are, but know not what we may be."

name: Fontaine, John E.
motto: "I'll put a girdle around about the earth In forty minutes."
text: "I'll put a girdle around about the earth In forty minutes."

name: Simmons, Donald J.
motto: "Oh, it is excellent to have a giant's strength."
text: "Oh, it is excellent to have a giant's strength."

name: Bailey, Hartwell Carr
motto: "A life of leisure and a life of happiness are two different things."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Senior Pharmacy Class
text: "A life of leisure and a life of happiness are two different things."

name: Cox, Herman E.
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man; Who dares do more, is none."
text: "I dare do all that may become a man; Who dares do more, is none."

name: Griffin, Willie Franklin
motto: "Another good plowhand ruined."
text: "Another good plowhand ruined."

name: Mixon, Edgar Allan
motto: "He was so good he would pour rosewater on a toad."
text: "He was so good he would pour rosewater on a toad."

name: Pentecost, James Andrew
motto: "Our content is our best having."
text: "Our content is our best having."

name: Rowan, Leon Franklin
motto: "Why, man. she is mine own; And I am rich in having such a jewel!"
text: "Why, man. she is mine own; And I am rich in having such a jewel!"

name: Simon, Abe
motto: "I am as free as nature first made man."
text: "I am as free as nature first made man."

name: Sisk, William Rodney
motto: "Many dreamed not to find, neither deserved And yet are steep'd in favors."
text: "Many dreamed not to find, neither deserved And yet are steep'd in favors."

name: Stovall, Mack G.
motto: "He trudged along, unknowing what he sought, And whistled as he went, for want of thought."
text: "He trudged along, unknowing what he sought, And whistled as he went, for want of thought."
